M-056.NAME: mtproto-analytics-service
M-056.owns: MTProto usage aggregation, top-user analytics, per-proxy summaries, runtime availability summaries, and observe-only abuse classification.
M-056.purpose: Convert raw MTProto telemetry into operator-safe analytics and abuse signals for the admin panel.

exports.fn-build-global-summary: Return issued proxy totals, assignment status counts, active connections, runtime health, and availability proof state.
exports.fn-build-assignment-usage: Return per-proxy last seen, connection count, duration, traffic, errors, active sessions, and abuse signals.
exports.fn-build-top-users: Rank users by traffic, connection count, duration, or error count for a selected period.
exports.fn-build-timeseries: Phase-43 graph buckets for traffic, connections, active connections, duration, and errors.
exports.fn-rollup-usage-windows: Maintain hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly aggregate windows from M-054 events.
exports.fn-detect-abuse-signals: Detect many IP hashes, excessive concurrency, traffic spikes, and repeated failures in observe-only mode.

M-056.evidence: pass-local 2026-05-19: analytics service tests and scheduler regression passed.

analytics-policy.policy-1: Abuse output starts as observe-only and never automatically disables an assignment in Phase-42.
analytics-policy.policy-2: Traffic spikes are compared against prior local baselines, not fixed global limits only.
analytics-policy.policy-3: Reports distinguish known assignment traffic from unknown or rejected SNI traffic.
analytics-policy.policy-4: Phase-43 high/critical abuse signals create M-060 admin alerts but still do not auto-disable access.

constraints.constraint-1: Aggregation must avoid N+1 database scans on admin pages.
constraints.constraint-2: Admin analytics must make missing telemetry explicit instead of silently showing zero usage.

M-056.hotspot: H-043: Abuse signals start observe-only until thresholds are tuned against real mobile-network behavior
